subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the concept of Rotational Symmetry
Identify the Center of Rotation and Angle of Rotation
Calculate the Order of Rotational Symmetry
Compare Line Symmetry vs. Rotational Symmetry in polygons
Recognize symmetry in 2D representations of 3D objects

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Rotational Symmetry
topics_covered: Turning a shape around a fixed point
What makes a shape look the same after a partial turn?
Real-life examples: Ceiling fans, wheels, windmills
student_activities: Trace a square and rotate it 90 degrees to check for fit
Identify the 'Center of Rotation' for various school logos
assessment: Identification of 'Yes/No' for rotational symmetry in shapes
Exit slip: 'What is the angle of rotation for a half-turn?'

lesson_plan.title: Order of Rotational Symmetry
topics_covered: Counting the number of 'fits' in one full 360-degree turn
Relationship between side count and order in regular polygons
teaching_strategy: Comparative chart (Square vs. Rectangle vs. Triangle)
student_activities: Calculate the order for a regular pentagon and hexagon
Create a table: Shape | Lines of Symmetry | Order of Rotation
assessment: Board activity matching shapes to their 'Order'

teacher_tip: If a shape only looks like itself after a full 360-degree turn, we say it has 'Order 1'—which basically means it has no special rotational symmetry.
